content_en:
The family, in the Islamic worldview, is the fundamental social institution — the building block of a healthy community and the primary environment for the moral and spiritual formation of individuals. Al-Qaradawi's chapter on the Sunnah and family life examines how prophetic tradition shapes every dimension of family relations: courtship and marriage, spousal rights and responsibilities, parenting, the treatment of the elderly, and the extended kinship networks that provide social cohesion in Muslim communities.

The Prophet's example in marriage is perhaps the most extensively documented personal dimension of his life. His long and devoted marriage to Khadijah, his patient and affectionate relationships with his subsequent wives, his explicit statements about the rights of women in marriage, and his practical guidance on resolving marital conflicts provide a comprehensive model that classical scholars have analyzed in great detail. The famous hadith 'The best of you are those who are best to their wives, and I am the best of you to my wives' (Tirmidhi) established a standard for marital conduct that challenged the cultural norms of the Prophet's time and continues to challenge many Muslim communities today.

Al-Qaradawi examines the Sunnah's vision of complementary roles within marriage. The Prophet established that the husband's primary responsibility is material provision (nafaqah) and protective leadership (qiwamah), while the wife's primary domain is the management of the home and the nurturing of children. These complementary roles do not imply the inferiority of women or the absolute authority of men; the Quran and Sunnah impose reciprocal obligations and grant reciprocal rights to both spouses. The Prophet's personal conduct — helping with household tasks, never striking any of his wives, consulting them on personal matters — demonstrated that male authority in the family must be exercised as service, not domination.

Parenting receives extensive treatment in the Sunnah. The Prophet loved children demonstrably — he interrupted sermons to pick up his grandchildren, played with them, and showed physical affection that was remarkable in a culture that prized stoic masculinity. His guidance on child-rearing combines gentleness with structure: 'Command your children to pray when they are seven years old, and discipline them for it when they are ten, and separate them in their beds' (Abu Dawud). This graduated approach to instilling religious practice reflects a sophisticated developmental understanding that modern educators might recognize.

The Sunnah's treatment of the elderly reflects the profound respect for parents that Islam instills. The Prophet ranked parental respect immediately after the worship of Allah and prohibited harming or even speaking rudely to parents. The famous hadith in which a man asks which deed is most beloved to Allah and is told 'prayer at its proper time,' then 'righteousness to parents,' before 'jihad in the way of Allah' (Bukhari and Muslim), establishes the family as the first arena of moral excellence.

Al-Qaradawi concludes the chapter by noting that the Sunnah's family model is not patriarchal oppression dressed in religious language but a carefully balanced system designed to provide stability, security, and love for all family members. The dysfunction visible in many Muslim families today reflects not adherence to prophetic example but departure from it.
content_id:
Keluarga, dalam pandangan dunia Islam, adalah institusi sosial yang paling fundamental — bata bangunan komunitas yang sehat dan lingkungan utama bagi pembentukan moral dan spiritual individu. Bab Al-Qaradawi tentang Sunnah dan kehidupan keluarga mengkaji bagaimana tradisi kenabian membentuk setiap dimensi hubungan keluarga: peminangan dan pernikahan, hak-hak dan tanggung jawab pasangan, pengasuhan anak, perlakuan terhadap orang tua yang lanjut usia, dan jaringan kekerabatan yang lebih luas yang memberikan kohesi sosial dalam komunitas-komunitas Muslim.

Teladan Nabi ﷺ dalam pernikahan mungkin merupakan dimensi pribadi dari kehidupannya yang paling banyak terdokumentasi. Pernikahannya yang panjang dan penuh pengabdian dengan Khadijah, hubungan-hubungannya yang sabar dan penuh kasih sayang dengan istri-istrinya berikutnya, pernyataan-pernyataannya yang eksplisit tentang hak-hak wanita dalam pernikahan, dan panduan praktisnya dalam menyelesaikan konflik pernikahan memberikan model komprehensif yang telah dianalisis oleh para ulama klasik secara mendetail. Hadis yang terkenal 'Sebaik-baik kalian adalah yang paling baik terhadap istrinya, dan aku adalah yang paling baik di antara kalian terhadap istriku' (Tirmidzi) menetapkan standar perilaku pernikahan yang menantang norma-norma budaya di zaman Nabi ﷺ dan terus menantang banyak komunitas Muslim hingga hari ini.

Al-Qaradawi mengkaji visi Sunnah tentang peran-peran yang saling melengkapi dalam pernikahan. Nabi ﷺ menetapkan bahwa tanggung jawab utama suami adalah pemberian nafkah materi dan kepemimpinan protektif (qiwamah), sementara domain utama istri adalah pengelolaan rumah tangga dan pengasuhan anak-anak. Peran-peran yang saling melengkapi ini tidak menyiratkan kerendahan perempuan atau otoritas mutlak laki-laki; Al-Quran dan Sunnah menetapkan kewajiban-kewajiban timbal balik dan memberikan hak-hak timbal balik kepada kedua pasangan. Perilaku pribadi Nabi ﷺ — membantu pekerjaan rumah tangga, tidak pernah memukul satu pun istrinya, berkonsultasi dengan mereka pada masalah-masalah pribadi — menunjukkan bahwa otoritas laki-laki dalam keluarga harus dijalankan sebagai pelayanan, bukan dominasi.

Pengasuhan anak mendapat pembahasan yang ekstensif dalam Sunnah. Nabi ﷺ mencintai anak-anak secara nyata — beliau menghentikan khotbah untuk menggendong cucu-cucunya, bermain bersama mereka, dan menunjukkan kasih sayang fisik yang luar biasa dalam budaya yang menghargai maskulinitas stoik. Panduan beliau tentang pengasuhan anak menggabungkan kelembutan dengan struktur: 'Perintahkan anak-anakmu shalat ketika berusia tujuh tahun, dan pukullah mereka jika meninggalkannya ketika berusia sepuluh tahun, dan pisahkan tempat tidur mereka' (Abu Dawud). Pendekatan bertahap dalam menanamkan praktik keagamaan ini mencerminkan pemahaman perkembangan yang canggih yang mungkin diakui oleh para pendidik modern.

Perlakuan Sunnah terhadap orang lanjut usia mencerminkan rasa hormat yang mendalam kepada orang tua yang ditanamkan Islam. Nabi ﷺ menempatkan penghormatan kepada orang tua tepat setelah ibadah kepada Allah dan melarang menyakiti atau bahkan berkata kasar kepada orang tua. Hadis terkenal di mana seorang laki-laki bertanya amal apakah yang paling dicintai Allah dan dijawab 'shalat pada waktunya', kemudian 'berbakti kepada kedua orang tua', sebelum 'jihad di jalan Allah' (Bukhari dan Muslim), menetapkan keluarga sebagai arena pertama keunggulan moral.

Al-Qaradawi menyimpulkan bab ini dengan mencatat bahwa model keluarga Sunnah bukanlah penindasan patriarkal yang dibalut bahasa agama, melainkan sistem yang seimbang dengan cermat yang dirancang untuk memberikan stabilitas, keamanan, dan kasih sayang bagi semua anggota keluarga. Disfungsi yang terlihat dalam banyak keluarga Muslim saat ini mencerminkan bukan kepatuhan pada teladan kenabian, melainkan penyimpangan darinya.
content_ar: The الأسرة, in the Islamic worldview, is the الأساسي social institution — the building block of a healthy الجماعة and the الأساسي environment for the moral and spiritual formation of individuals. Al-Qaradawi's الباب on the السنة and الأسرة الحياة examines how prophetic التراث shapes every dimension of الأسرة relations: courtship and marriage, spousal الحقوق and responsibilities, parenting, the treatment of the elderly, and the extended kinship networks that provide social cohesion in مسلم communities. النبي ﷺ's example in marriage is perhaps the most extensively documented personal dimension of his الحياة. His long and devoted marriage to Khadijah, his patient and affectionate relationships with his subsequent wives, his explicit statements about the الحقوق of women in marriage, and his practical الهداية on resolving marital conflicts provide a comprehensive model that الكلاسيكي العلماء have analyzed in great detail. The famous الحديث 'The best of you are those who are best to their wives, and I am the best of you to my wives' (الترمذي) established a standard for marital السلوك that challenged the cultural norms of النبي ﷺ's time and continues to challenge many مسلم communities today. Al-Qaradawi examines the السنة's vision of complementary roles within marriage. النبي ﷺ established that the الزوج's الأساسي المسؤولية is material provision (nafaqah) and protective leadership (qiwamah), بينما the الزوجة's الأساسي domain is the management of the home and the nurturing of الأولاد. These complementary roles do not imply the inferiority of women or the absolute authority of men; the القرآن الكريم and السنة impose reciprocal obligations and grant reciprocal الحقوق to both spouses. النبي ﷺ's personal السلوك — helping with household tasks, never striking any of his wives, consulting them on personal matters — demonstrated that male authority in the الأسرة must be exercised as service, not domination. Parenting receives extensive treatment in the السنة. النبي ﷺ loved الأولاد demonstrably — he interrupted sermons to pick up his grandchildren, played with them, and showed physical affection that was remarkable in a culture that prized stoic masculinity. His الهداية on الولد-rearing combines gentleness with structure: 'Command your الأولاد to pray عندما they are seven years old, and discipline them for it عندما they are ten, and separate them in their beds' (أبو داود). This graduated approach to instilling religious practice reflects a sophisticated developmental الفهم that modern educators might recognize. The السنة's treatment of the elderly reflects the profound الاحترام for الوالدان that الإسلام instills. النبي ﷺ ranked parental الاحترام immediately بعد the worship of الله and prohibited harming or even speaking rudely to الوالدان. The famous الحديث in which a man asks which deed is most beloved to الله and is told 'الصلاة at its proper time,' then 'righteousness to الوالدان,
